filter_index.video_title: Introducing The Miyar Valley Trek in Lahaul | Indiahikes | Good Treks in Lahaul and Spiti

snow_or_rain_notes: Snow patches on trail in Summer (June-July) with hard snow closer to Miyar Glacier
No snow on trek in Monsoon except at Miyar Glacier
Monsoon season (August-September) brings moist conditions and rain/drizzle that lowers temperatures
Summer rain can drop daytime temperatures to around 15 °C
temperature_notes: Monsoon (August-September) daytime: 15-18 °C; nighttime: 0-5 °C, dipping to 0 °C in September
3 warm layers required in both seasons

terrain_challenges: Long trekking days of 7-8 hours covering 9-10 km daily at ~12,000 ft
Multiple river/stream crossings including glacial streams with freezing cold water
Boulder sections requiring hopping, skipping, and balancing
Uneven trails with daily ascents and descents
Steep uphill climb right after Khanjer leading to a high-altitude cricket ground (gateway into Miyar Valley); described as tiring and requiring proper pacing
altitude_or_weather_risks: Trek starts high at 11,564 ft and climbs to 13,202 ft, making trekkers susceptible to altitude sickness in the first couple of days
Risk of Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) above 10,000 ft applies to both first-time and experienced trekkers; even fit people climbing too high too soon are at equal risk
Early season (June) brings a lot of snow on the trail, making it tricky
July and August rain is common, making boulder sections slippery
Heavy rains significantly increase water force at river crossings, making them more challenging

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 5 km in under 35 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
approval_requirements: Screenshots must demonstrate meeting the trek's fitness criteria
Runs must meet the trek's fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month
prep_window: 6 weeks (free 6-week training plan designed by Prathima Chhabria and Gautam Singh)

experience_themes: Summer (June-July) is dry and crisp with snow patches on the trail and hard snow closer to Miyar Glacier
Monsoon (August-September) is moist with no snow on the trek except at Miyar Glacier
Snow patches on ground above 10,000 feet in Summer after winter has left the slopes
Snow patches on trail in Summer (June-July) with hard snow closer to Miyar Glacier
No snow on trek in Monsoon except at Miyar Glacier
primary_watchouts: Night temperatures dip to near zero in both seasons
Rain or drizzle in Monsoon lowers temperatures further
Keep a jacket handy during Monsoon
Long trekking days of 7-8 hours covering 9-10 km daily at ~12,000 ft
Multiple river/stream crossings including glacial streams with freezing cold water
Boulder sections requiring hopping, skipping, and balancing
